Commercial display rule: The report shows full modeled operational savings first and an illustrative 50% Glyph Systems cut separately. The split is a planning illustration, not a contractual entitlement. Customer-retained value, audit rules, delivery costs, and the final performance percentage require written agreement and real-data validation.
Evidence boundary: This is a controlled matched recovery benchmark for a high-volume payment network using compressed representative processing units. Modeled savings represent avoided operational friction and waste, not guaranteed liquid cash, realized customer savings, or Glyph Systems revenue. No market prediction, trading, fee selection, account access, or electrical-energy claim is included.

Executive result

Logic Engine and the industry best-practice baseline processed the same transactions, service outcomes, physical capacity, queue limits, and incident schedule across 72 complete independent ten-minute rounds. The cumulative full modeled operational savings were $41,964.71, equal to $58.2843 per minute. The illustrative Glyph Systems cut was $20,982.35, equal to $29.1422 per minute.

The complete-round distribution had a median of $8.8897/min, a 10th percentile of $2.1042/min, and a worst round of $1.3129/min.

Methodology and fairness

Each complete round lasts ten minutes: twenty seconds of clean stable-state calibration, randomized operational incidents and recovery windows, and a one-minute final clean drain. The first 72 rounds are precommitted to 22 NORMAL NETWORK TRAFFIC, 24 REGIONAL LOAD PRESSURE, 18 MULTI-REGION RECOVERY INCIDENT, and 8 PEAK NETWORK CAPACITY EVENT rounds. Later rounds use deterministic randomized 3/3/3/1 blocks.

All five controls receive identical transactions, hidden service outcomes, incident schedule, physical service ceiling, queue limit, retry limit, idempotency capability, visible state source, restoration timing, and frozen cost coefficients. Controls differ only in scheduling, holding, verification, retry timing, isolation, retained-state use, and recovery-policy interpretation.

Program 41 uses 70 simulation units per second, 120-unit clean capacity, and a 40,000-unit queue. Each simulation unit represents 100 production events for transparent scale reporting only. Cost assumptions are frozen before the run and represent a high-volume network operating-cost model. Controllers cannot access the accounting model.

The adaptive-state track carries only clean stable-state estimates between rounds. Transactions, queues, active incident state, accumulated system pressure, unresolved recovery load, recovery-hold state, and staged-release state reset every round.

First-72 profile-and-seed commitment: 8280c91ae83f62b3ea76d6de3d2b6be0e0353c054abd09ff8ae310696939d4a9.
